title: Lastrea squamigera
intro: Lastrea squamigera, also known as the Indian fern, is a type of fern plant that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea squamigera include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or powder, and is also used in traditional medicines, such as decoctions and ointments, to treat a range of health issues.
taxonomy: Lastrea squamigera is classified within the family Athyriaceae. This species belongs to the genus Lastrea and is a type of fern. The classification places it in the class Polypodiopsida and the division Pteridophyta. It is also known as the scaly lady fern due to its distinctive scaly rhizome. The family Athyriaceae is a group of ferns commonly found in Asia.
uses: Lastrea squamigera is traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Lastrea squamigera is used to treat digestive problems and to reduce inflammation in the body. Additionally, the plant is said to have antioxidant properties, which help protect against cell damage and promote overall health. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential benefits and limitations of using Lastrea squamigera for medicinal purposes.
